code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean subject name (remove disambiguators like (footballer))
    def clean_name(name):
        return name.split(" (")[0] if " (" in name else name

    # Build subject-to-triples mapping to group related information
    subject_triples = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subj = triple.subject
        if subj not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[subj] = []
        subject_triples[subj].append(triple)

    # Phrasing templates for predicates - more natural language
    phrasing_templates = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"stands {o} tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"weighs {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"plays in the {o} league",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"played for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"plays as a {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"is based in {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is from {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"participated in the {o} season",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"was founded on {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"is in the {o} time zone",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"covers an area of {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people are called {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"speaks {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"official language is {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"anthem is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"leader is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"most champions are {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"currency is {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"plays at {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"stadium is {o}",
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"serves {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"was drafted by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"was draft pick {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"was draft round {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"debuted with {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"previously played for {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"wears number {o}",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"started at {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"started in {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"general manager is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"chairman is {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"plays for {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"includes {o}",
    }

    def generate_clause(triple, is_first=False, connector=""):
        pred = triple.predicate.lower()
        subj = clean_name(triple.subject)
        obj = triple.object

        if pred in phrasing_templates:
            verb_phrase = phrasing_templates[pred](subj, obj)
            if is_first:
                return f"{subj} {verb_phrase}"
            else:
                # Don't repeat subject for same main entity
                return f"{connector} {verb_phrase}"
        else:
            if is_first:
                return f"{subj} {pred} {obj}"
            else:
                return f"{connector} {pred} {obj}"

    # Build the sentence by connecting related triples
    all_clauses = []
    main_subject = None

    # Find the most common subject (likely the main entity)
    for subj, subj_triples in subject_triples.items():
        if len(subj_triples) > 0:
            main_subject = subj
            break

    if main_subject:
        # Generate clauses for main subject first
        main_clauses = []
        other_clauses = []

        for triple in triples:
            if triple.subject == main_subject:
                main_clauses.append(triple)
            else:
                other_clauses.append(triple)

        # Process main subject clauses
        for i, triple in enumerate(main_clauses):
            is_first = (i == 0)
            connector = "and" if not is_first else ""
            clause = generate_clause(triple, is_first, connector)
            all_clauses.append(clause)

        # Process related entity clauses with proper relative clauses
        for triple in other_clauses:
            pred = triple.predicate.lower()
            subj = clean_name(triple.subject)
            obj = triple.object

            if pred in phrasing_templates:
                verb_phrase = phrasing_templates[pred](subj, obj)
                # Create proper relative clause connecting to main subject
                if main_subject and subj == clean_name(main_subject):
                    # Same subject, just add the verb phrase
                    clause = f"{verb_phrase}"
                else:
                    # Different subject - create relative clause
                    if "played" in verb_phrase or "attended" in verb_phrase or "started" in verb_phrase:
                        clause = f"who {verb_phrase}"
                    elif "is" in verb_phrase or "plays" in verb_phrase:
                        clause = f"which {verb_phrase}"
                    else:
                        clause = f"and {subj} {verb_phrase}"
                all_clauses.append(clause)
            else:
                all_clauses.append(f"{subj} {pred} {obj}")

    # Join into a coherent sentence - avoid "and and" duplication
    # Clean up any leading connectors from clauses
    cleaned_clauses = []
    for clause in all_clauses:
        if clause.lower().startswith("and "):
            cleaned_clauses.append(clause[4:])
        elif clause.lower().startswith("which "):
            cleaned_clauses.append(clause)
        elif clause.lower().startswith("who "):
            cleaned_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            cleaned_clauses.append(clause)

    if len(cleaned_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = cleaned_clauses[0] + "."
    elif len(cleaned_clauses) == 2:
        # Check if second clause already starts with relative pronoun
        if cleaned_clauses[1].lower().startswith("which ") or cleaned_clauses[1].lower().startswith("who "):
            sentence = cleaned_clauses[0] + " " + cleaned_clauses[1] + "."
        else:
            sentence = cleaned_clauses[0] + ", and " + cleaned_clauses[1] + "."
    else:
        # Join with proper comma and "and" structure
        # Separate relative clauses from main clauses
        main_clauses = []
        relative_clauses = []
        for clause in cleaned_clauses:
            if clause.lower().startswith("which ") or clause.lower().startswith("who "):
                relative_clauses.append(clause)
            else:
                main_clauses.append(clause)

        if main_clauses:
            sentence = ", ".join(main_clauses[:-1])
            if main_clauses[-1].lower().startswith("and "):
                sentence += " " + main_clauses[-1] + "."
            else:
                sentence += ", and " + main_clauses[-1] + "."
        else:
            sentence = cleaned_clauses[0] + " " + ", ".join(cleaned_clauses[1:]) + "."

        # Add relative clauses
        for rel_clause in relative_clauses:
            if "which" in rel_clause.lower() or "who" in rel_clause.lower():
                if not sentence.endswith("."):
                    sentence = sentence[:-1] + ", " + rel_clause + "."
                else:
                    sentence = sentence[:-1] + ", " + rel_clause + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
